topic: China SG null (2009) — verify communiqés → communiqués typo fix (WIN-014.c9 H0)
change_summary: One-character typo fix in detail_evidence: missing 'u' restored in 'communiqés' → 'communiqués' (codepoint 75 reinserted between 71 and e9). The typo had been introduced by the original ISS-1829 / EXP-323 patch that added the hedged 2013 CJG/CAS attribution.
our_argument_summary: WIN-014 (verdict: Refuted by Data — Self-Contradicted) argues that the dome cannot simultaneously claim the 2009 Chinese SG null result confirms the model and predict a positive 10–13 µGal contact-phase signal for the August 2026 SG test (κ-cluster contradiction). The c9 H0 typo was a one-codepoint defect introduced by EXP-323's hedge insertion (ISS-1829) and is the sole scope of queue_id 347.

verification_evidence.hexdump_at_token: 0x63 0x6f 0x6d 0x6d 0x75 0x6e 0x69 0x71 0x75 0xe9 0x73 — 'c o m m u n i q u é s' — codepoint 0x75 ('u') is present between 0x71 ('q') and 0xe9 ('é'), matching the original EXP-323 replacement_html and standard French orthography.
verification_evidence.note: Length delta of exactly +1 character matches the typo-fix scope (one codepoint added). docs/index.html also contains the correct form exactly once and zero typos.

relevant_files: raw-text/monitor.py Domain #7 (κ self-coupling, China SG context)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Not re-inspected this run — pure prose typo fix. Dome's automation surface unchanged.
data_sources_used: Sun et al. 2013 CJG paper (doi:10.6038/cjg20130306) — already verified by ISS-1337 and the EXP-323 attribution patch
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None introduced by the typo fix.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Unchanged from c9 — monitor.py Domain #7 fetches data and compares to the κ-cluster prediction; queue_id 347 is a prose-only fix.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Unchanged from c9 — the WIN-014 / WIN-012 κ-cluster contradiction is intrinsic to the dome's own equations; not a relabel of standard physics.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Unchanged from c9 — the 2009 China SG null result was published 2010–2013, well before the dome added it as a 'confirmed' WIN.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Unchanged from c9 — the 10–13 µGal prediction derives from the dome's published κ = 1.677 nT/µGal coupling; the contradiction with the 2009 null is precisely what makes WIN-014 Self-Contradicted.

holes_found.description: tldr_evidence still reads 'The eight-gravimeter network, sampling continuously at 0.1–1 µGal precision, recorded zero across all sites.' — a definite-article phrasing that does not reflect the EXP-323 / ISS-1829 hedge that the detail_evidence now carries: 'a network of approximately eight superconducting/spring gravimeces across roughly six Chinese sites ... (the station count and site count trace only to that 2013 CJG record and contemporaneous CAS communiqués; we have not located an independent English-language verification).' This was flagged in WIN-014.c9 as hole [1] with a note that it was 'optional, low-priority' — it is still standing, no issue was opened, and a careful reader who clicks expand will notice the TLDR claims more confidence about the campaign structure than the hedged detail.
holes_found.recommendation: Soften tldr_evidence sentence 3 to: 'The Chinese SG network — described by the 2013 CJG record as approximately eight superconducting/spring gravimeters across roughly six sites — recorded zero across all sites.' OR keep the simpler phrasing but change 'eight-gravimeter network' to 'Chinese SG network' to remove the precise station count from the TLDR. Adds 30–50 characters to the current 412-char tldr_evidence.
holes_found.classification_note: Standing finding from WIN-014.c9 hole [1] — not introduced by this run. Flagged again because no open issue exists and c10's batch-verification scope brings it within view.

kernel_of_truth.description: The typo fix is exactly the kind of low-cost cleanup that should be done promptly (data/wins.json:014.detail_evidence). The original EXP-323 patch was correctly conceived (hedge the campaign structure to the 2013 CJG paper + CAS communiqués) and the typo was an easily-corrected accidental byte-loss during JSON replacement. Worth noting that the decider's choice to push this as queue_id 347 with class='verification' rather than letting it ride to the next holistic review correctly distinguishes 'verify a patch landed' from 'fresh adversarial argumentation' — the kind of class-discrimination PROP-025 introduced (monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-025*).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Typo-fix verifications do not bear on the WIN-014 verdict, which remains 'Refuted by Data — Self-Contradicted' on independent κ-cluster grounds (WIN-014 + WIN-012 + WIN-013 cannot all simultaneously hold). Nothing about the orthography of 'communiqués' touches the κ-cluster argument.

deeper_analysis: This is the third consecutive batched verification on WIN-014 (q339 c8 + q345 c9 + q347 c10). Per the curmudgeon.md Step 8a anti-staleness guard, the last singleton-deep review was c9 (queue_id 345, not marked batched) one cycle ago — under the 3-cycle threshold, so batching is permitted. However, three consecutive batched cycles is starting to look like a pattern the operator should be aware of; if WIN-014 attracts a fourth batched verification before a fresh deep dive, the anti-staleness gate (gate 4 in Step 8a) will trip on the next run and force a singleton-deep review. That would be appropriate — WIN-014 carries the load-bearing Self-Contradicted verdict and deserves periodic fresh adversarial attention rather than perpetual minor-patch verification. Surfacing this in summary_for_decider so the queue scheduler can plan.

citations_verified: EXP-323 / ISS-1829 hedge was correctly drafted with 'communiqués' (codepoints 0x71 0x75 0xe9 0x73) in the replacement_html; the typo appeared during application/serialization and has now been corrected back to the EXP-323 source spelling
citations_unchecked: κ-cluster cross-WIN consistency — out of scope for queue_id 347 verification

summary_for_decider: Typo fix landed cleanly: detail_evidence now has 'communiqués' (correct, codepoint sequence 0x71 0x75 0xe9 0x73) exactly once and zero occurrences of 'communiqés' (the typo, missing 0x75 'u'). docs/index.html also correct. One MINOR finding carries over from c9 H1: tldr_evidence still uses unhedged 'eight-gravimeter network' phrasing that doesn't match the EXP-323 hedge in detail_evidence — no open issue tracks this. Recommend either opening a minor issue or treating it as a 'soften TLDR to match hedged detail' batch with the WIN-006 TLDR finding from c10. Process note: WIN-014 is on its third consecutive batched verification (q339, q345, q347); a fourth in a row would trip the 3-cycle anti-staleness guard on the next push and force singleton — which would be a healthy fresh look at the κ-cluster argument.
